title: Nonlocal transport phenomena in coupled quasiperiodic Kitaev chains
abstract: We investigate the topological phases in a coupled one-dimensional p-wave superconducting Fibonacci quasicrystal modeled by the quasiperiodic Kitaev chain. Recent studies have shown that the coupled system can host topological edge modes with Majorana fermions and enhance their topological protection, depending on the pattern of quasiperiodicity. In this work, we elucidate the topological phases of the coupled system and demonstrate the dependence of differential conductance on the lead connecting pattern employing the Keldysh formalism and the recursive Green's function method. Our findings reveal the emergence of topological phases in the coupled system, which are characterized by the presence of Majorana edge modes and the seepage of the Majorana wave function. Furthermore, we identify a new topological phase transition induced by quasiperiodicity in the coupled system.
summary: This paper explores how Fibonacci quasiperiodicity influences the topological properties of coupled Kitaev chains. It reveals that while Majorana signatures like zero-bias peaks are robust, quasiperiodicity can induce new phase transitions and cause asymmetric Majorana wave-function seepage between the chains.
main_problem: The study investigates topological phase transitions and nonlocal transport phenomena in coupled quasiperiodic Kitaev chains, specifically focusing on how Fibonacci quasiperiodicity affects Majorana edge modes and wave-function seepage.
main_result: The authors identify new topological phase transitions induced by quasiperiodicity and discover asymmetric Majorana wave-function seepage between chains, while finding that zero-bias conductance peaks remain robust against quasiperiodic modulation.
method: The study employs the Keldysh formalism and the recursive Green's function method to calculate differential conductance, alongside the pseudospectral method to determine topological indices.
model_or_system: A coupled one-dimensional p-wave superconducting Fibonacci quasicrystal consisting of a superconducting (S) chain and a normal (N) chain, modeled via a Bogoliubov-de Gennes Hamiltonian with interchain hopping and pairing.
key_observables: Local and nonlocal differential conductance, zero-bias conductance peaks (ZBCP), and the topological index (nu).
important_parameters: Interchain hopping (tau_int), quasiperiodicity parameters (rho_S, rho_N), chemical potentials (mu_S, mu_N), and the ratio of hopping to pair potential (alpha).
main_assumptions: The energy gap is determined by the average value of the real-space pair potential, and the system is treated within a tight-binding framework with open boundary conditions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the schematic of the coupled system; Figure 2 compares differential conductance for various lead connection patterns in periodic and quasiperiodic cases; Figure 6 illustrates topological phase transitions; Figure 8 compares ZBCP in periodic and quasiperiodic chains.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the definition of the coupled Hamiltonian and quasiperiodic model, proceeds to derive the analytical framework for conductance using Keldysh Green's functions, analyzes topological invariants via the pseudospectral method, and concludes with a discussion on the equivalence to s-wave proximity-induced systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic drawing of the coupled system. The ar- rows represent the hopping between the nearest-neighbor and interchain sites in this figure. The ellipses represent the cou- pling with the nearest-neighbor and interchain nearest site. If this system has quasiperiodicity, then nearest-neighbor hop- ping and coupling depend on the distance between neighbor sites.

FIG. 2. The differential conductance of all lead connecting patterns. Q and P denote the quasiperiodic and periodic, respectively. For the periodic case, we set ρS = ρN = 1.0. For the quasiperiodic case, we set ρS = 1/τ 2 g and ρN = 1.0. Other parameters are set as ∆int = tint = 1.0, cS = 10, cN = 0.8, α = 0.3, µ = 0.0 and N = 145 (11th generation).

FIG. 3. Topological phase transition of the coupled system with periodic chains. The parameters are set as ρS = ρN = 1.0, cS = 10.0, cN = 1.0 and α = 0.3. (a) is the topological invariant of this system. The red, white, and blue regions correspond to the topological phases with ν = 2, 1, and 0, respectively. (b) The energy spectrum with periodic bound- ary conditions is shown with a logarithmic scale. (c) is the local differential conductance of the system renormalized by e2/2h. The labels SL, SR, NL, and NR correspond to the left edge of the superconducting chain, the right edge of the superconducting chain, the left edge of the normal chain, and the right edge of the normal chain,...
FIG. 4. Topological phase transition of the coupled system with quasiperiodic chains. The parameters are set as ρS = ρN = 1/τ 2 g and α = 0.3. What is shown in each panel is the same as in Fig. 3.
FIG. 5. Topological phase transition of the coupled system with quasiperiodic chains with α = 1.0. Other parameters are the same as in Fig. 4. What is shown in each panel is the same as in Fig. 3.
